title: Supply chain: unpinned production dependency versions allow silent upgrades
risk_statement: All 6 production dependencies (ajv, ajv-formats, chalk, commander, fs-extra, inquirer) used caret (^) ranges in package.json, permitting npm to resolve newer minor/patch versions than those recorded in the lockfile. A compromised minor-version release of any dependency could execute arbitrary code on developer machines via postinstall hooks or payload injection.

acceptance.reason: Risk mitigated in v2.2.0: all 6 production dependencies pinned to exact versions. .npmrc enforces save-exact=true. CI uses npm ci. prepublishOnly runs npm audit --audit-level=high.

mitigations.detail: All 6 production dependencies pinned to exact versions in package.json (no ^ or ~ ranges).
.npmrc sets save-exact=true. CI installs via npm ci --ignore-scripts to enforce the lockfile.
prepublishOnly script runs npm audit --audit-level=high, blocking publish on any high/critical finding.
